title: task
description:
## Summary

Define the human-readable node address format for LICHEN.

## Requirements

- Short enough to write, speak, type from memory
- Collision-resistant at planetary scale (5B nodes)
- Cryptographically bound to node identity (Ed25519 pubkey)
- Compatible with IPv6 IID for routing

## Design

### Derivation

```
Ed25519 pubkey (256 bits)
        |
    SHA-256
        |
    First 64 bits --> IID (for IPv6 routing)
        |
    Base32 encode --> 13 chars (for humans)
```

### Format

```
Short (human):   KCVN-MRPX-QWERT    (13 chars, dashes every 4)
Full (routing):  fd00:lichen:xxxx:xxxx:<IID>
Verify (auth):   full Ed25519 pubkey (on first contact)
```

### Base32 Alphabet

Use Crockford's base32 (excludes confusables 0/O, 1/I/l):
`0123456789ABCDEFGHJKMNPQRSTVWXYZ`

### Collision Analysis

| Nodes | Expected collisions | Acceptable? |
|-------|---------------------|-------------|
| 15M   | ~0.0001 pairs       | Yes         |
| 1B    | ~0.03 pairs         | Yes         |
| 5B    | ~0.5 pairs          | Yes         |

### Collision Resolution

If two nodes share short address (rare), resolve on contact:
1. Both respond to lookup
2. Requester disambiguates via GPS, context, or pubkey verification
3. Full pubkey exchange on first message establishes binding

## Files to Update

- spec/03-addressing.md (or create if missing)
- Add examples to spec/appendix-*.md
- Update any IID references in routing specs

## Test Vectors

Generate 10 example keypairs with their derived addresses for cross-impl testing.
close_reason: Defined IPv6 formats (link-local fe80::IID, ULA fd00:lichen::IID, Yggdrasil 02xx::/IID from SHA256(Ed25519 pubkey)), implemented lichen_pubkey_to_iid + updates to CoAP keys/app_identity/tests/spec/04-network.md, verified with updated tests. Human short Base32 pending follow-up bead.
